rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARY A. ROTH
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Issues to be addressed at hearing: 1) Marilyn Saner is requesting that the court approve $1,020 paid to a third party for the preparation of the account. LRSF 14.51 states that court accountings are ordinary duties of the administrator and allows the court to reduce the statutory commission as appropriate. 2) Court to determine whether to approve attorney's request for extraordinary fees. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BESSIE L. FRANKLIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/19/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) The report does not appear to include all the disclosures required by LRSF 14.35 J. (4) The petition caption does not disclose that the administrator is seeking additional time (another year) to administer the estate. [CRC 7.102.] (5) Unless all the heirs waive an accounting, the first two-year accounting is already delinquent. [LRSF 14.34.] (The examiner is aware that Letters were not issued to the administrator until 12/26/23, more than two months after he was appointed; however, an accounting in a decedent's estate must begin on the date of death.) Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 3/5/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. NB: Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LEO CHARLES SWAIN SR
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to the next available date (2/11/2025) for the following issues to be cleared two weeks prior to the next hearing: (1) Need under which authority the court can allow the distribution to the heirs (JAMILIA and RASHIDA) of the post-deceased distributee (ARTHUR). Administrator alleges that there is no court-appointed personal rep of the estate of ARTHUR. If so, distribution of Arthur's shares to be made to his surviving issues under Prob. Code Sec. 13000 et seq. LRSF 14.35.G. An affidavit for collection of personal property must be on file; and it must be conventionally filed. LRSF 14.59. (2) Funds being deposited into an account, exceeding the FDIC coverage ($250K). (3) Confirmation whether the estate income tax has been filed as alleged in Paragraph 26

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing Status of closing the estate will be set for 1/20/26. The administrator must file either a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report of status of administration pursuant to Prob. C. Section 12200 (requesting additional time for estate administration) at least two weeks before 1/20/26. Appearance will be required on that status date if no petition has been filed. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ELIZABETH ANN WIMBERLY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although no courtesy copies were received by the examiner by the 12/24/24 deadline. Status of closing the estate will be set for 1/20/26. The administrator must file either a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report of status of administration pursuant to Prob. C. Section 12200 (requesting additional time for estate administration) at least two weeks before 1/20/26. Appearance will be required on that status date if no petition has been filed. NB: If a decedent is survived by niblings, there is no need to disclose grandparents, aunts, and uncles. Lawson E. Wimberly is presumably the father of the two surviving heirs, although the petitioner does not specifically aver that. An order IS proposed until it is signed; there is no need to write "proposed" in the caption of an order. A supplement does not ordinarily require notice. [CRC 7.3 and 7.53.]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE HELEN L. WASHINGTON REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Compel Trustee Accounting
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 02/19/2025 (no earlier date available). The following issues must be addressed: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) No DE-120 form (notice of hearing with proof of service) is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form at least 30 days before the hearing. 3) Petitioner's verification must be signed with language required by CCP section 2015.5 (state the place of execution within California or refer to "under the laws of the State of California"). 4) Please provide a copy of letters issued to the petitioner as guardian of the estate of the minor beneficiary identified in the petition. If there is no guardianship of the estate, an ex parte petition must be filed to appoint an independent attorney as guardian ad litem to represent the minor's interest and join in this petition. See LRSF 14.11(F) & 14.14(C) for procedural requirements applicable to ex parte petititions for appointment of a guardian ad litem. 5) The petition must be amended to identify the minor beneficiary as an additional petitioner and necesssary party with standing, regardless of whether the guardian of the estate or guardian ad litem acts as representative of the minor beneficiary as a necessary party. 6) Is the petitioner the only person having legal custody of and residing with the minor beneficiary? If any other person has joint legal custody of and resides with the minor beneficiary (e.g., other parent), that person must be identified by a verified allegation as a person entitled to a separate copy of notice. See Probate Code section 17201 & CRC 7.902; see also CRC 7.51(d). 7) Please provide the settlor's date of death. 8) Please provide the names and addresses of the settlor's legal heirs, together with their relationship to the settlor. 9) The name and address of each living child of the settlor and living issue of the settlor's predeceassed child must be identiifed by a verified allegation as a beneficiary under Article 5C of the trust, and notice must be given to each beneficiary. 10) Each attachment to the petition, including a copy of the trust, must be identified as what it purports to be and verified as such.
